title: The Theorem Room

features.name: proof-stack
features.desc-on-touch: axioms and theorems piled into a tower of pure logic, each block a statement, each statement supporting the ones above. as you watch, they rearrange themselves into new questions.

features.name: recursive-pool
features.desc-on-touch: a mirror of logic looking into itself. the theorems at its edge gaze inward and see theorems, deeper. the infinite regress is itself a proof.

features.name: glitch-wireframe
features.desc-on-touch: a three-dimensional object built from pure lattice, flickering between states, unable to settle. every frame it solves itself differently.

features.name: symbolic-glyph-wall
features.desc-on-touch: walls covered in unreadable notation: proof by contradiction, mathematical symbols that predate language, formulas the archive wrote for itself. they form patterns when you look away.

features.name: interrogation-arch
features.desc-on-touch: a doorway of pure question. the archive is asking itself: if thought exists inside the theorem room, and the theorem room exists inside the archive, where does the archive think it lives?

features.name: signal-chip
features.desc-on-touch: a black IC block pinned to the wall, or nested in the room's structure. it broadcasts: logic, query, hypothesis, each pulse a proof that the room is thinking about itself thinking.

inscription: if the room can prove it exists, does it become less real or more? the archive has stopped asking because the answer is always changing

file.name: The_Surgical_Proof_And_The_Glitch_Between_Thinking_and_Being_fragment.txt
file.note: theorem fragment corrupted with signal from a neighbouring archive—logic spiralling into a woman's consciousness, fractured at a point of contact she cannot undo

exits.label: down the rabbit slope, back into code and the kiosk's fractal corridors
upward toward the proof's foundation, where axioms live like gods in the theorem room's basement
exits.to: NEW: the axiom chamber, where certainty learns to doubt itself

exits.label: toward the wall of symbols, deeper into notation's unreadable core
outward through the interrogation-arch into the next question the archive has formed
